name: Octopus Studio
description: A canonical month-long org where the Octopus team uses Octopus to build, operate, and grow Octopus itself.

organization.name: Octopus Studio
organization.description: Internal Octopus operating org: agents build the product, run release reliability, operate growth, triage messenger intake, and leave a natural work-history trail for Calendar.

goals.title: Increase completed real agent-work loops
goals.description: Make Octopus's own development team complete more real end-to-end agent work loops every week.

goals.title: Make Calendar prove the work history
goals.description: Calendar should reveal how agents actually spent time across product, growth, release, support, and decisions without creating fake calendar-only scenarios.

goals.title: Reach qualified agent-team builders
goals.description: Turn Octopus growth into concrete public shipping, distribution, DM follow-up, and feedback loops with measurable qualified interest.

projects.name: Agent Work Loop
projects.description: Tighten the issue -> assignment -> run -> review -> close loop that defines Octopus's north star.

projects.name: Calendar as Work History
projects.description: Show real agent operation history and planned human checkpoints as a calendar view over the same org activity.

projects.name: Desktop Release Reliability
projects.description: Make local desktop boot, packaged smoke, profile isolation, and release verification boring.

projects.name: Developer Platform & Skills
projects.description: Improve repo-local skills, agent adapters, plugin docs, and reusable workflows for contributors.

projects.name: Marketing & Growth
projects.description: Concrete growth work: public posts, demos, DM follow-up, community launch prep, feedback synthesis, and weekly growth reporting.

projects.name: Messenger & Issue Intake
projects.description: Turn messy operator chats, requests, and approvals into auditable issues without losing context.

projects.name: Operator Experience
projects.description: Make dashboards, issue triage, approvals, and run intelligence feel like a control room for agent teams.
